skill.name: nanobanana
skill.description: Generate, edit, and restore images with Google's Nano Banana (Gemini image models). Use whenever the user asks to "generate an image", "create an icon/favicon/logo", "edit this photo", "restore an old photo", "make a pattern/texture/wallpaper", "draw a diagram/flowchart/architecture", or "tell a visual story" — even when they don't explicitly say Nano Banana or Gemini. Always prefer this skill over describing images in text. Requires NANOBANANA_API_KEY (or GEMINI_API_KEY) env var.
skill.summary: Generate images via Gemini AI with support for editing, restoration, icons, patterns, diagrams, and storytelling.
skill.author: batou9150

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 216 potential issues but manual evaluation confirms all are false positives. The skill is a legitimate image generation tool using Google's Gemini API. Network calls go exclusively to Google's official API endpoints. Environment variable access is standard API key configuration. Subprocess usage is limited to safe desktop integration (image preview). Base64 decoding handles image data from API responses. No credential exfiltration, data leakage, or malicious behavior detected.

content.user_title: Generate Stunning Images with Gemini AI
content.value_statement: Creating visuals for projects takes time and design skills. This skill connects Claude directly to Google's Gemini image models so you can generate, edit, and restore images through simple natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text descriptions using Gemini AI
Edit existing images with natural language instructions
Restore and enhance old or damaged photos
Create app icons, favicons, and UI elements at multiple sizes
Generate seamless patterns, textures, and wallpapers
Create diagrams, flowcharts, and architecture visualizations
Generate sequential story frames for narratives and tutorials
limitations: Requires a Google Gemini API key (free tier available at aistudio.google.com)
Output is saved to local filesystem, cannot directly embed in documents
Content safety filters may block certain image requests

use_cases.title: Quick Visual Prototyping
use_cases.description: Generate multiple image variations instantly to explore design directions without opening Photoshop or Figma.

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Create architecture diagrams, flowcharts, and wireframes to illustrate system designs in documentation.

use_cases.title: Asset Creation for Projects
use_cases.description: Generate icons, favicons, and UI elements at required sizes for applications and websites.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of {your description here}
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a single image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 watercolor style variations of {your subject}

prompt_templates.title: Icon or Favicon
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a {style} style {type} for {your app name} with sizes 16, 32, 64, and 256 pixels
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need app icons at multiple resolutions

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Draw a {type} diagram for {your system} showing {key components} with {style} style

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a cozy coffee shop interior with natural lighting
Create a flat style favicon for a note-taking app
Generate an architecture diagram for a microservices app with clean professional style

best_practices: Be specific in your image descriptions including style, mood, and key elements
Use the specialized subcommands (icon, pattern, diagram, story) for better results with specific use cases
Set the NANOBANANA_API_KEY environment variable once in your shell configuration for persistent access
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like "make something cool" - specify the subject and style
Do not ask for complex scenes with too many subjects - focus on one main concept per generation
Avoid requesting content that may trigger safety filters - keep descriptions appropriate

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Get a free API key from https://aistudio.google.com/apikey. The free tier has generous limits for image generation.

faq.question: Where should I store my API key?
faq.answer: Add it to your ~/.zshenv (or ~/.bash_profile) as: export NANOBANANA_API_KEY="your-key". This makes it available to Claude Code's shell sessions.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill works as a Claude Code plugin. Install with: /plugin marketplace add batou9150/nanobanana-skill

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill can save images as PNG, JPEG, WebP, and GIF depending on the Gemini model response.

faq.question: Where are images saved?
faq.answer: All generated images are saved to ./nanobanana-output/ in your current working directory.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --count flag with the generate command. You can also combine --styles and --variations for batch generation.
